title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Haemul Pajeon: Authentic Korean Seafood Pancake Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Haemul Pajeon, the crispy Korean seafood pancake, at home. This easy recipe brings the taste of Korea to your kitchen!
longTailKeywords: best Haemul Pajeon recipe at home
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon: Korean Seafood Pancake Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Haemul Pajeon
Pro Tips for the Best Haemul Pajeon
Serving Suggestions for Your Korean Seafood Pancake
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Haemul Pajeon Korean seafood pancake with dipping sauce on a plate
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a freshly made, crispy Haemul Pajeon, a beloved Korean seafood pancake perfect for any meal.

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, crispy on the outside and tender within, packed with fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. It's a delightful appetizer or side dish, perfect for sharing and a true taste of Korean street food culture. Enjoy this flavorful dish with a tangy dipping sauce for an unforgettable culinary experience.

ingredients.notes: peeled, deveined, chopped into bite-sized pieces
cleaned, body sliced into thin rings
or other neutral oil, for frying

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Clean and chop the shrimp into bite-sized pieces. Clean the squid and slice the body into thin rings. Cut green onions into 2-3 inch lengths and thinly slice the yellow onion. If using, thinly slice the red chili.

instructions.title: Make the Pancake Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, corn starch, salt, one large egg, and very cold water until just combined. A few lumps are fine; do not overmix, as this can lead to a tough pancake.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, and gochugaru (if using). Stir well until the sugar dissolves. Set aside.

instructions.title: Cook the First Pancake
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or griddle over medium-high heat. Add a generous amount of vegetable oil, ensuring the pan is well coated. Once the oil is hot, pour about 1/2 to 3/4 cup of batter into the pan, spreading it thinly with the back of a ladle to form a round pancake.

instructions.title: Add Toppings and Cook
instructions.text: Immediately arrange a good mix of shrimp, squid, green onions, sliced yellow onion, and red chili (if using) evenly over the wet batter. Pour a small amount of additional batter over the toppings to help bind them. Drizzle a bit more oil around the edges of the pancake. Cook for 4-5 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Flip and Finish Cooking
instructions.text: Carefully flip the Haemul Pajeon with a wide spatula. Press down gently to ensure even contact with the pan. Cook for another 3-4 minutes until the other side is also golden brown and crispy, and the seafood is fully cooked through.

instructions.title: Repeat and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked pancake to a cutting board. Repeat the process with the remaining batter and toppings, adding more oil as needed for each pancake. Slice the Haemul Pajeon into wedges and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

tips: For the crispiest Haemul Pajeon, ensure your pan is hot and use very cold water for the batter. Do not overmix the batter; a few lumps are perfectly fine.
Feel free to customize your seafood mix. Mussels, oysters, or small pieces of firm white fish can also be excellent additions. Just ensure all seafood is fresh and cut to a manageable size.
The dipping sauce is key! Adjust the balance of sweet, sour, and spicy to your preference. A touch of minced garlic or ginger can elevate its flavor even further.
article:
Craving a taste of Korea that's both comforting and exciting? Look no further than Haemul Pajeon, the iconic Korean seafood pancake. This beloved dish offers a delightful symphony of textures and flavors, combining a crispy exterior with a soft, savory interior packed with fresh seafood and vibrant green scallions. Whether you're a seasoned Korean food enthusiast or new to its culinary wonders, Haemul Pajeon is an absolute must-try that promises to transport your taste buds straight to the bustling streets of Seoul. Perfect for sharing as an appetizer, a hearty snack, or even a light meal, this savory pancake is a staple in Korean households and restaurants alike, celebrated for its deliciousness and versatility.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?

Haemul Pajeon is a beloved savory Korean pancake, distinguished by its generous inclusion of fresh seafood (haemul) and an abundance of green onions (pa). The word "jeon" refers to a dish made by pan-frying ingredients coated in an egg and flour batter, and pajeon specifically highlights the green onions. What makes Haemul Pajeon so special is the harmonious blend of sweet, succulent seafood – typically shrimp, squid, mussels, or oysters – embedded within a tender, slightly chewy batter, all punctuated by the crisp, slightly pungent bite of scallions. It’s often enjoyed during rainy days in Korea, paired with a refreshing glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine), creating a cozy and convivial atmosphere. The unique preparation method, which often involves arranging the scallions and seafood artfully before pouring the batter, contributes to its visually appealing presentation and distinct texture.

## Ingredients You Need for Haemul Pajeon

To create the perfect Haemul Pajeon, you'll need a mix of fresh, high-quality ingredients. The beauty of this dish lies in its adaptability, so feel free to adjust the seafood to your preference.

For the Pancake Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour (or a mix of all-purpose and glutinous rice flour for extra chewiness)
*   1/2 cup cold water or anchovy broth (for deeper flavor)
*   1 large egg
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   Pinch of black pepper

For the Filling:
*   1 bunch (about 6-8) large green onions, cut into 3-4 inch lengths
*   1/2 lb mixed seafood, such as shrimp (peeled, deveined, cut into bite-sized pieces), squid (cleaned, scored, cut into rings or strips), mussels (cleaned), or oysters.
*   1/4 cup finely chopped onion (optional, for added sweetness)
*   1-2 fresh red or green chili peppers, thinly sliced (optional, for a kick)

For Frying:
*   3-4 tablespoons vegetable oil (or any neutral oil)

For the Dipping Sauce (Ganjang Yangnyeomjang):
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1/2 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1/2 teaspoon minced garlic
*   Pinch of red chili flakes (gochugaru)

## Step-by-Step Haemul Pajeon Instructions

Making Haemul Pajeon at home is surprisingly straightforward and incredibly rewarding. Follow these steps for a delicious result:

1.  **Prepare the Dipping Sauce**: Combine all dipping sauce ingredients in a small bowl and set aside. This allows the flavors to meld.
2.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables**: If using frozen seafood, thaw it thoroughly and pat dry. Cut all seafood into bite-sized pieces. Wash and trim the green onions, cutting them into approximately 3-4 inch lengths. If using, thinly slice the chili peppers and finely chop the onion.
3.  **Make the Batter**: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, cold water/broth, egg, salt, and pepper until just combined. Be careful not to overmix; a few lumps are fine. The batter should be the consistency of a thin pancake batter, easily pourable but thick enough to coat the ingredients.
4.  **Heat the Pan**: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the entire bottom of the pan. The pan needs to be hot enough to create a crispy crust.
5.  **Assemble the Pancake**: Once the oil is shimmering, arrange half of the green onion pieces in a single layer across the bottom of the hot pan. Place half of the mixed seafood evenly over the green onions. If using, scatter half of the chopped onion and chili slices.
6.  **Pour the Batter**: Ladle about half of the prepared batter evenly over the seafood and green onions, ensuring it covers most of the ingredients and fills any gaps. Gently press down with a spatula to ensure everything is submerged and cohesive.
7.  **Cook the First Side**: Cook for 4-6 minutes, or until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to set. You can peek underneath with a spatula.
8.  **Flip the Pancake**: Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake. Carefully slide a large, flat spatula underneath the pancake. With a swift, confident motion, flip the Haemul Pajeon over. Alternatively, you can slide it onto a plate, then invert the pan over the plate and flip it back into the pan.
9.  **Cook the Second Side**: Cook for another 3-5 minutes, or until the second side is also golden brown and crispy, and the seafood is cooked through.
10. **Serve**: Carefully slide the cooked Haemul Pajeon onto a cutting board. Cut it into wedges or squares. Repeat the process for the remaining ingredients. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce.

## Pro Tips for the Best Haemul Pajeon

Achieving that perfect balance of crispy exterior and tender interior for your Haemul Pajeon requires a few insider tricks:

*   **Cold Water/Broth is Key**: Using very cold water or chilled broth for your batter helps create a crispier pancake. The temperature shock when it hits the hot pan contributes to a light, airy texture.
*   **Don't Overmix the Batter**: Overmixing develops gluten, which can make your pancake tough. Mix just enough until the ingredients are combined, leaving a few lumps.
*   **Hot Pan, Generous Oil**: A properly heated pan with sufficient oil is crucial for a crispy, golden crust. Don't skimp on the oil; it helps fry the pancake rather than just cooking it.
*   **Even Distribution**: Arrange your green onions and seafood evenly in the pan before pouring the batter. This ensures every bite has a good mix of ingredients.
*   **Pat Seafood Dry**: Excess moisture from the seafood can make your pancake soggy. Pat shrimp, squid, etc., thoroughly dry with paper towels before adding them to the pan.
*   **Serve Immediately**: Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the pan when it's at its crispiest and hottest.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Korean Seafood Pancake

Haemul Pajeon is a versatile dish that can be enjoyed in several ways. The most traditional and arguably best way to serve it is hot, cut into bite-sized pieces, alongside its accompanying soy-vinegar dipping sauce. This sauce provides a tangy, savory counterpoint to the rich pancake.

It makes an excellent appetizer for any Korean meal, complementing dishes like kimchi jjigae (kimchi stew) or bulgogi. For many Koreans, Haemul Pajeon is synonymous with rainy days, and it's traditionally paired with makgeolli, a milky, slightly sweet Korean rice wine. The refreshing effervescence of makgeolli beautifully cuts through the richness of the pancake. You can also serve it as a light lunch or dinner, perhaps with a side of kimchi or pickled radishes to cleanse the palate.

## Haemul Pajeon Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Haemul Pajeon is a culinary delight, there are many ways to customize it to your taste or dietary needs:

*   **Mixed Seafood**: Feel free to experiment with different combinations of seafood. Clams, small scallops, or even imitation crab meat can be added. Ensure all seafood is fresh and cut to a similar size for even cooking.
*   **Vegetarian/Vegan Options**: For a vegetarian version, omit the seafood and add more vegetables like mushrooms (shiitake or oyster), carrots, zucchini, or bell peppers. For a vegan option, use a plant-based egg substitute or simply omit the egg from the batter, relying on a slightly thicker flour-water mixture.
*   **Meat Additions**: Some variations include thinly sliced beef (e.g., bulgogi-style beef) or pork alongside or instead of seafood, creating a "Gogi Pajeon" (meat pancake) or a "Haemul Gogi Pajeon" (seafood and meat pancake).
*   **Kimchi Pajeon**: For a spicy, tangy twist, add finely chopped kimchi to the batter. This is another popular Korean pancake, offering a completely different flavor profile.
*   **Gluten-Free**: Replace all-purpose flour with a gluten-free flour blend (e.g., rice flour, tapioca starch, potato starch mix) to make a celiac-friendly version.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a pancake; it's an experience. Its irresistible combination of crispy edges, tender interior, and flavorful seafood makes it a dish that appeals to almost everyone. Whether you stick to the traditional recipe or venture into creative variations, making this Korean seafood pancake at home is a rewarding journey that brings a piece of Korean culinary magic into your kitchen. So, gather your ingredients, heat up your pan, and get ready to enjoy one of Korea's most beloved comfort foods!
